perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Affiliate" of a Party means any Person, whether de jure or de facto, which directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such Person for so long as such control exists, where "control" means the decision-making authority as to such Person and, further, where such control shall be presumed to exist where a Person owns more than fifty percent (50%) of the equity (or such lesser percentage which is the maximum allowed to be owned by a foreign corporation in a particular jurisdiction) having the power to vote on or direct the affairs of the entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Affiliate" of a Party means any Person, whether de jure or de facto, which directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such Person for so long as such control exists, where "control" means the decision-making authority as to such Person. Control can also be established where a Person has a strategic alliance with such Party. Further, where such control shall be presumed to exist where a Person owns more than twenty-five percent (25%) of the equity (or such lesser percentage which is the maximum allowed to be owned by a foreign corporation in a particular jurisdiction) having the power to vote on or direct the affairs of the entity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of "Affiliate" in Section 1.3. The original text defines control based on over 50% equity ownership. The modified text broadens this to include strategic alliances and lowers the equity ownership threshold to 25%. This contradicts other sections of the contract that rely on the original definition to determine rights and obligations and creates uncertainity in cases where strategic alliances will be considered affiliates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 2 RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS 2.1 License Grants from Theravance to GSK. 2.1.1 Development License. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, including without limitation Section 2.2, Theravance grants to GSK, and GSK accepts, an exclusive (except as to Theravance and its Affiliates) license in the Field under the Theravance Patents, Theravance Know-How and Theravance's rights in the Joint Inventions to make, have made, use and Develop Collaboration Products for Commercialization in the Territory. 2.1.2 Commercialization License. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, including without limitation Section 2.2, Theravance hereby grants to GSK, and GSK accepts, an exclusive license in the Field under the Theravance Patents, Theravance Know-How and Theravance's rights in the Joint Inventions to make, have made use, sell, offer for sale and import Collaboration Products in the Territory. 2.1.3 Manufacturing License. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, including without limitation Section 2.2, Theravance grants to GSK an exclusive license in the Field under the Theravance Patents, Theravance Know-How and Theravance's rights in the Joint Inventions to make and have made API Compound or formulated Collaboration Product in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of 'Affiliate' in Section 1.3 creates a contradiction with Article 2, which grants licenses to GSK, with exceptions for Theravance and its Affiliates. The original definition relied on a 50% equity ownership. The modified text broadens this to include strategic alliances and lowers the equity ownership threshold to 25%. This broadens the scope of 'Affiliates,' altering the exclusivity granted to GSK. The change introduces uncertainty about who is considered an affiliate, which directly impacts the exclusive licenses granted to GSK under Article 2.1.1, 2.1.2 and 2.1.3, creating a strong in-text contradiction because a strategic alliance or an entity with only 25% ownership now falls under the definition of Affiliate.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3.1 Royalty on Single-Agent Collaboration Products and LABA/ICS Combination Products. Within twenty (20) days after the end of each Calendar Quarter , GSK shall pay Theravance royalty payments based on Net Sales in such Calendar Quarter during the Term as follows: On total Annual Worldwide Net Sales up to and including U.S. $3 Billion: 15 % On total Annual Worldwide Net Sales greater than U.S. $3 Billion: 5 % it being understood that Net Sales of a single agent Collaboration Product will be combined with Net Sales of a LABA/ICS Combination Product for purposes of the foregoing royalty calculation. The quarterly royalty payments made under this Section 6.3.1 may be based on estimated Net Sales. Within thirty (30) days after the end of each Calendar Quarter, GSK shall calculate the actual amount of Net Sales for the previous Calendar Quarter and either credit or debit the difference between such actual and projected amount on the succeeding Calendar Quarter's royalty payment to Theravance. As soon as practical following the end of each Calendar Month, but in no event later than the 10 business day of the following month, GSK will provide Theravance with an estimate of Net Sales for such Calendar Month.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3.1 Royalty on Single-Agent Collaboration Products and LABA/ICS Combination Products. Within thirty (30) days after the end of each Calendar Quarter , GSK shall pay Theravance royalty payments based on Net Sales in such Calendar Quarter during the Term as follows: On total Annual Worldwide Net Sales up to and including U.S. $3 Billion: 15 % On total Annual Worldwide Net Sales greater than U.S. $3 Billion: 5 % it being understood that Net Sales of a single agent Collaboration Product will be combined with Net Sales of a LABA/ICS Combination Product for purposes of the foregoing royalty calculation. The quarterly royalty payments made under this Section 6.3.1 may be based on estimated Net Sales. Within twenty (20) days after the end of each Calendar Quarter, GSK shall calculate the actual amount of Net Sales for the previous Calendar Quarter and either credit or debit the difference between such actual and projected amount on the succeeding Calendar Quarter's royalty payment to Theravance. As soon as practical following the end of each Calendar Month, but in no event later than the 10 business day of the following month, GSK will provide Theravance with an estimate of Net Sales for such Calendar Month.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction in payment deadlines for royalty reports. The original text provides two different deadlines for the same action. It states that royalty payments should be made within twenty (20) days, but then changes to thirty (30) days for GSK to calculate the Net Sales. This contradiction will creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which payment deadline should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.8 Interest on Late Payments. If either Theravance or GSK shall fail to make a timely payment pursuant to this Article 6, any such payment that is not paid on or before the date such payment is due under this Agreement shall bear interest, to the extent permitted by applicable law, at the average one-month London Inter-Bank Offering Rate (LIBOR) for the United States Dollar as reported from time to time in The Wall Street Journal, effective for the first date on which payment was delinquent and calculated on the number of days such payment is overdue or, if such rate is not regularly published, as published in such source as the Joint Steering Committee agrees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction in the payment deadlines for royalty reports. The original text states royalty payments should be made within twenty (20) days, while the changed version introduces a thirty (30) day deadline for GSK to calculate Net Sales. This directly conflicts with section 6.8, which discusses interest on late payments. Since there are now two different deadlines, it's unclear when a payment would be considered late, thus directly contradicting and impacting section 6.8.
